translation: And Jesse begot David the king. And David begot Solomon by the [woman] of Uriah,

alternate_translations.translation: Jesse was the father of David the king. David was the father of Solomon by Uriah’s wife,
alternate_translations.notes: “ἐκ τῆς τοῦ Οὐρίου” is an incomplete phrase in the provided text; in this genealogy context it refers to Solomon being born from Uriah’s wife (commonly identified as Bathsheba), so supplying “wife” makes the sense explicit while preserving the meaning.

alternate_translations.translation: And Jesse fathered David the king. And David fathered Solomon through Uriah’s [wife],
alternate_translations.notes: “ἐκ” (“by/from”) can be rendered as “through,” indicating the maternal connection. As in the primary translation, the wording is truncated in the source, so “wife” is supplied for clarity based on the common biblical usage.

notes: This is Greek (from a genealogy). The phrase ends mid-sentence (“…ἐκ τῆς τοῦ Οὐρίου,”), so the full thought is not present in the excerpt. Grammatically, the genitive feminine article (“τῆς…”) typically indicates a woman associated with Uriah; in the wider scriptural context (Matthew 1:6), this is understood to be Uriah’s wife (Bathsheba).
